title: Discounted Pit-Loss EV From SC/VSC Risk
summary: Public F1 strategy material consistently notes that pit loss collapses under Safety Car or VSC, and teams explicitly plan around caution probability; a next-lap pit model should depend on expected pit-cost discount, not only tyre age and finishability.
rationale: Public F1 strategy material consistently notes that pit loss collapses under Safety Car or VSC, and teams explicitly plan around caution probability; a next-lap pit model should depend on expected pit-cost discount, not only tyre age and finishability.
implementation_hint: Create per-race priors for green-flag pit loss and SC/VSC discount factors, then form features such as expected pit loss, caution-adjusted undercut value, and 'wait-for-cheap-stop' pressure by interacting those priors with progress, position, and service-window pressure.
expected_effect: Improves ranking on tracks where optimal behavior is to stretch stints while waiting for a likely cheap stop, especially late-stint borderline cases.
risk: Caution priors are approximate and may inject noise if circuit mappings are crude or not year-aware.
